response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned web and full-stack software engineer with over 20 years of experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed systems, but his profile is entirely misaligned with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, and GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His AI-related work is limited to software-layer agentic tooling (MCP frameworks), not hardware performance or deep-learning workload optimization, and his educational background (BS in CS, BS in Photography/Design) does not offset the experience gap in the required technical domain.
strengths: 20+ years of broad engineering experience including low-latency, high-throughput systems at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Exposure to AI-adjacent tooling and agentic infrastructure (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) demonstrates awareness of the AI ecosystem
Early C/C++ work at National Instruments and embedded/robotics work at Altom shows some hardware-adjacent systems programming
concerns: Zero experience in SoC/IP modeling, hardware performance analysis, or AI accelerator design — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, deep neural network training/inference optimization, or any hardware-software co-design
Principal-level hardware architecture roles typically require advanced degrees (MS/PhD) or equivalent domain-specific depth; candidate holds only a BS and no hardware specialization

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No deep-learning workload or GPU architecture expertise
